turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, doctor, I've been really struggling lately. I've gained almost ten kilos in the past six months and I haven't changed my eating or exercise habits at all. On top of that, I'm exhausted all the time - I can barely get through my workday as an office manager without needing to sit down. And my hair is falling out more than usual, which is really upsetting me.
When did this start?
It's been about six months now, maybe a little longer. The weight gain sort of crept up on me, but the tiredness and hair loss have been more noticeable over the last three or four months.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

I don't really have sharp pain anywhere, it's more like a dull, heavy feeling all over, especially in my legs and lower back. It's like my body feels weighed down and sore, particularly after I've been standing or walking for a while.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills at all. Just the tiredness and the weight gain, nothing like that.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not taking any prescription medications right now. I do take a multivitamin sometimes, but not every day. And I occasionally take ibuprofen if my back feels especially sore, maybe once or twice a week.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

Not that I know of. I've never been diagnosed with anything serious like diabetes or thyroid problems, but I haven't had a full check-up in a few years either. That's partly why I wanted to come in today, to see what might be going on.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I'm not allergic to any medications that I know of. I've taken things like ibuprofen and antibiotics in the past without any problems.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've been trying to rest more and take my multivitamin more regularly, but honestly, nothing seems to help. I thought maybe if I just pushed through the fatigue it would get better, but it hasn't. I also tried cutting back on carbs for a few weeks, but the scale didn't budge at all, which just made
Does anything make it better or worse?

Actually, resting does help a little with the tiredness, but I still feel heavy and sluggish even after a full night's sleep. Stress seems to make everything worse—if I have a busy day at work, I feel more drained and my lower back aches more. But nothing really makes it better permanently, if that makes sense.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been really hard, to be honest. At work, I find myself needing to sit down every hour or so during my shift, which isn't like me at all. I used to be the one running around the office all day. At home, I've lost interest in cooking and even just going for walks with my husband, and that's made me feel pretty down about myself. The hair loss especially is getting to me—I
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke at all. I do have a glass of wine maybe once or twice a week with dinner, but nothing more than that. No other drugs or anything like that.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

My mother has always struggled with her weight and had high blood pressure, but nothing quite like this. My maternal grandmother had thyroid problems, I think she was on some kind of medication for it. My father's side doesn't have much history that I know of.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, never. I've always been a pretty healthy and active person for my age, so this is all very strange and upsetting for me. I've never had trouble with my weight or felt this kind of tiredness before, even during stressful times.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say maybe a 7. It's not unbearable pain, but the constant heaviness and soreness really wears me down day after day. Emotionally, I'd say an 8—I feel really down about myself, maybe even
Is there anything else you think I should know?
I think that covers the main
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Not very well, honestly. I try to put on a brave face at work and for my husband, but most days I just feel like crying. I've been avoiding looking at myself in the mirror because of the weight gain and hair loss, and I don't have much motivation for things I used to enjoy. I guess I'm just hoping you can help me figure out what's wrong so I can feel like myself again.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Oh, I think I already mentioned that, but I'll add—it's been a struggle to stay positive. Some days I just go through the motions at work and come home to crash on the couch. My husband has been supportive, but I feel guilty for being so down all the time. I'm really hoping there's a medical reason for all this so I can get treatment and start feeling better.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I'm sorry if I'm repeating myself, but it's just hard. I'm struggling, but I'm here because I want to take the first step to getting better. I trust you'll be able to help.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I think I've covered that already, but the main thing is I'm not coping well—I just want to feel like myself again. I'm really hoping we can find out what's causing all these changes.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I understand you're asking again, but I think I've shared how I'm feeling—pretty down and worn out. The main thing is I'm hoping you can help me get to the bottom of this. I'm ready to do whatever tests or changes you recommend.
